notes: TO type registry for the NY&E Northern Lights Subdivision dispatcher UI.
Types are single-purpose — prototype dispatchers may combine types in one TO, but the NY&E issues one type per order.
Each type defines the fields the dispatcher fills in and the text template the system renders.
addressed_to is always manually selected by the dispatcher from the 7-station list.
Template variables: {field_id} substitutes the field value directly; computed vars are listed under template_vars.
Rendering notes are in the 'notes' field of each type.
default_form is set by the management module. The dispatcher UI stamps every TO with this value; no per-TO selection.

meet.label: Meet Order (Form A)
meet.description: Fix a meeting point for two opposing trains (Standard Code Form A). The order establishes where; timetable rights determine who takes the siding.

fields.label: Train B number / engine
fields.help: Train number for scheduled trains; engine number for extras

template_vars.station_name: station name looked up from station field
template_vars.direction_b_word: North or South derived from direction_b

wait.label: Wait Order
wait.description: Direct a train to take a siding and wait until a specified time
wait.template: No. {train} Eng {engine} take siding at {station_name} and wait until {until_time}.

template_vars.station_name: station name looked up from station field

running_extra.label: Running Extra Authorization
running_extra.description: Authorize an engine to operate as a running extra train

fields.label: Departure Time (RR)
fields.help: For schedule generation — goes on the clearance form, not in the order text

template_vars.direction_word: North or South derived from direction
template_vars.from_station_name: station name looked up from from_station
template_vars.to_station_name: station name looked up from to_station

running_extra.notes: departure_rr_time is included in the MQTT payload for schedule generation (trains/extra/{engine}/schedule) but does not appear in order text. The extra schedule is published by RR_Server when this TO is issued.

work_extra.label: Work Extra Authorization
work_extra.description: Authorize an engine to perform work between two stations within a time window. All opposing trains receive TOs holding them clear of the section.
work_extra.template: Engine {engine} is authorized to work between {from_station_name} and {to_station_name} from {start_rr_time} to {end_rr_time}. All trains must be clear of this section during this time.

template_vars.from_station_name: station name looked up from from_station
template_vars.to_station_name: station name looked up from to_station

annulment.label: Annulment
annulment.description: Cancel a scheduled train for this session

fields.label: Train
fields.help: Timetable train number (engine assignment not needed)

fields.label: From Station
fields.help: For partial annulment only — leave blank to annul entire run

fields.label: To Station
fields.help: For partial annulment only — leave blank to annul entire run

template_vars.partial_suffix: if both from_station and to_station are present: ' {from_station_name} to {to_station_name}' — otherwise empty string

annulment.notes: Engine number is not required. Annulments are typically issued before or early in a session when the train has not yet assembled. Issuing an annulment triggers a trains/yard/notification type 'annulment' message to the Yardmaster.

sections.label: Train Sections
sections.description: Authorize a scheduled train to run in multiple sections
sections.template: No. {train} Eng {engine} will run in {section_count} sections.
sections.notes: Addressed to all stations on the train's route. Section numbers for subsequent sections are communicated via consist assignment (Yardmaster terminal) — this TO establishes authority for the full set of sections.
